summary: The workflow failed when attempting to push an update branch because the nextjs-bot account was denied permission to push to the vercel/next.js repository (HTTP 403), causing git to exit with code 128 and the job to fail.
root_cause: The push was rejected due to insufficient or invalid credentials for nextjs-bot: the remote returned HTTP 403 and git exited with code 128 when attempting to push the branch.

evidence.quote: remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.
evidence.reason: Shows the remote explicitly denied permission to the nextjs-bot account.

evidence.quote: fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403
evidence.reason: Confirms the HTTP 403 authentication/authorization error when accessing the repository URL.
Shows the failing git push command that attempted to push the update branch.
Indicates git returned a non-zero exit code on the failed push, causing the job failure.

evidence.quote: An error occurred while minifying. The result will not be minified.
evidence.reason: Present in the log but not directly tied to the authentication failure; noted as a separate/minification-related message.

suggested_fix: Update or replace the credentials used by nextjs-bot for this workflow: ensure the token has push permissions to the vercel/next.js repository (repo scope or appropriate repo-level access), or add/restore nextjs-bot's write access. After updating the token/permissions, re-run the sync-react workflow to verify the push succeeds.
